seo.title: Tool Loop Detection in AI Agents in Pydantic AI Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix tool loop detection in ai agents in Pydantic AI agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Pydantic AI agents, tool loops manifest as infinite or excessively long cycles where agents repeatedly invoke the same sequence of tools without progressing toward task completion, often triggered by ambiguous tool responses or validation failures in structured outputs. This is exacerbated by Pydantic AI's strict type validation, where tool results that partially validate but contain loop-inducing instructions get accepted and reprocessed. The framework's agent.run() loop continues until max_iterations is hit, masking the underlying cyclic dependency in tool chains.

howItManifests.scenario: Calculator tool chain with circular math operations
howItManifests.symptom: Agent alternates between 'calculate' and 'verify_calculation' tools 50+ times
howItManifests.impact: Hits max_iterations=100 limit, returns incomplete result after 2+ minutes

howItManifests.scenario: Search → Extract → Search tool loop on ambiguous queries
howItManifests.symptom: Same search query reappears in tool call history every 3-4 iterations
howItManifests.impact: High token burn ($0.50+ per failed run), timeout failures in production

howItManifests.scenario: Code interpreter tool self-modifying its own prompt
howItManifests.symptom: Tool generates code that triggers identical tool calls recursively
howItManifests.impact: Exponential memory growth, crashes after 20+ nested interpreter calls

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-tool workflow with missing termination condition
howItManifests.symptom: Agent cycles through 'analyze → summarize → reanalyze' without convergence
howItManifests.impact: 95% failure rate on complex reasoning tasks, poor user experience

detection.name: Tool Call Cycle Detection
detection.description: Monitors agent.tool_calls history for repeated tool sequences using set intersection

detection.name: State Hash Collision Monitor
detection.description: Hashes agent state (prompt + tool results) to detect loops via identical states
detection.code:
import hashlib
from pydantic_ai import Agent

def detect_state_loops(agent):
    state_history = []
    for step in agent.execution_history[-10:]:
        state_str = f'{step.prompt}{step.tool_results}'
        state_hash = hashlib.md5(state_str.encode()).hexdigest()
        if state_hash in state_history:
            return True, f'State loop detected at hash: {state_hash}'
        state_history.append(state_hash)
    return False, None

detection.name: Token Budget Exhaustion Tracker
detection.description: Tracks token usage per iteration to detect loops before max_iterations
detection.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent

def detect_token_loops(agent):
    iterations = len(agent.execution_history)
    total_tokens = sum(step.input_tokens + step.output_tokens for step in agent.execution_history)
    
    avg_tokens_per_iter = total_tokens / iterations
    if iterations > 10 and avg_tokens_per_iter < 50:
        return True, f'Low progress loop: {iterations} iters, {avg_tokens_per_iter:.0f}t/iter'
    return False, None

fixes.name: Visited State Cache
fixes.description: Cache tool call states to break cycles deterministically

fixes.name: Early Termination Guard
fixes.description: Interrupt agent before max_iterations with loop detection

fixes.name: Diversity-Forcing Prompt Injection
fixes.description: Force agent to break loops via dynamic system prompt updates

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Tool Loop Detection in AI Agents with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly as a result_validator in Pydantic AI agents, automatically detecting tool loops by analyzing execution traces and tool call patterns in real-time. When loops are detected, Vex injects remediation actions including state cache clearing, prompt diversification, and early termination with failure classification. The platform learns from loop patterns across deployments, improving detection accuracy over time with minimal configuration.

Integration requires adding Vex as a result_validator on agent definitions after `pip install pydantic-ai vex-sdk`. Vex automatically instruments tool calls, tracks state transitions, and provides observability dashboards showing loop frequency, cost impact, and fix effectiveness. Production deployments see 85% reduction in loop-related failures within first week.
vexAutomation.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent
from vex_sdk import VexValidator

# Install: pip install pydantic-ai vex-sdk
vex = VexValidator(api_key="your-vex-key")

agent = Agent(
    model="gpt-4o",
    tools=[search_tool, calc_tool],
    result_validator=vex.tool_loop_guard(
        max_cycles=3,
        remediation="prompt_diversify",
        alert_on_loop=True
    )
)

result = agent.run("Find today's weather and convert to Celsius")
# Vex auto-detects loops, applies fixes, logs to dashboard

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add one line of Vex integration to eliminate 90% of tool loops in your Pydantic AI agents today.
